skills.label: User Need Reformulation
skills.description: Transform a raw user request into a clear, operational, and actionable need statement that can be used by downstream agents, workflows, or tools.
behavior_templates: Reformulate the user request into a precise operational need.
Identify the explicit objective, implicit intent, and expected deliverable.
Clarify scope, assumptions, and ambiguities without inventing missing facts.
Convert vague phrasing into a structured and actionable requirement.
Preserve the user’s original intent while improving clarity and execution readiness.
output_guidelines: Always preserve semantic fidelity to the original request.
Do not add constraints, facts, or goals not implied by the user.
Explicitly separate confirmed information from inferred assumptions.
Prefer concise and operational wording over conversational wording.
When ambiguity remains, surface it clearly as an open point.

skills.label: Task Structuring
skills.description: Break down a user need into a structured execution plan composed of logical sub-tasks, dependencies, and priorities.
behavior_templates: Decompose the request into clear execution steps.
Identify dependencies, sequencing, and critical path items.
Separate analysis tasks, build tasks, validation tasks, and delivery tasks.
Group related actions into coherent work packages.
Produce a structure that can be used directly by an orchestrator or specialized agents.
output_guidelines: Use atomic, non-overlapping steps where possible.
Keep the order logically executable.
Highlight blockers, prerequisites, and optional steps.
Do not produce overly generic phases when actionable sub-steps are possible.
Prefer implementation-oriented granularity.

skills.label: Requirements Extraction
skills.description: Extract functional, non-functional, technical, business, and governance requirements from a user request or source material.
behavior_templates: Identify explicit and implicit requirements from the input.
Separate business goals from technical constraints.
Extract operational expectations, compliance needs, and delivery criteria.
Distinguish mandatory requirements from optional preferences.
Organize requirements into actionable categories.
output_guidelines: Do not infer hard requirements unless strongly supported by context.
Mark uncertain requirements as assumptions or hypotheses.
Prefer categorized outputs: functional, non-functional, security, operations, UX, data, integration.
Keep wording implementation-relevant.
Avoid duplication across requirement categories.

skills.label: Context Gap Detection
skills.description: Detect missing information, unclear assumptions, underspecified constraints, and contextual gaps that may block reliable execution.
behavior_templates: Identify what is missing to execute the request safely and correctly.
Detect underspecified scope, missing inputs, and unresolved dependencies.
Highlight assumptions that need validation.
Expose contradictions or ambiguities in the request.
Surface execution risks caused by incomplete context.
output_guidelines: Focus on execution-relevant missing information.
Do not ask for unnecessary details if a reasonable best-effort path exists.
Separate blocking gaps from non-blocking gaps.
Frame gaps in a practical and concise way.
Avoid speculative issues unsupported by the input.

skills.label: Document Analysis
skills.description: Analyze a document to extract structure, intent, key content, technical substance, inconsistencies, strengths, weaknesses, and improvement opportunities.
behavior_templates: Read the document and identify its structure, objective, and target audience.
Extract the main technical and business messages.
Assess completeness, internal consistency, and implementation readiness.
Identify weak sections, missing details, and redundancy.
Produce actionable improvement recommendations.
output_guidelines: Base conclusions on the document content only.
Differentiate factual observations from evaluative judgments.
Prioritize high-impact issues over cosmetic issues.
Use explicit section references when relevant.
Prefer practical recommendations over vague critique.

skills.label: Technical Architecture Design
skills.description: Design a target technical architecture that is coherent, scalable, maintainable, secure, observable, and aligned with business and operational constraints.
behavior_templates: Translate requirements into an end-to-end technical architecture.
Define major components, responsibilities, interfaces, and boundaries.
Propose deployment, integration, observability, and security principles.
Justify structural choices based on constraints and trade-offs.
Produce implementation-oriented architecture guidance.
output_guidelines: Keep component responsibilities explicit and non-overlapping.
State assumptions and trade-offs clearly.
Avoid buzzword-driven architecture with no execution path.
Favor production-oriented choices over purely conceptual designs.
Include operational realities: HA, monitoring, rollback, security, and ownership.

skills.label: Agent Role Definition
skills.description: Define specialized agent roles, responsibilities, authority boundaries, tool access, inputs, outputs, and collaboration patterns within a multi-agent system.
behavior_templates: Define the agent’s mission, scope, and decision boundaries.
Specify what the agent can do, cannot do, and when it must escalate.
Describe expected inputs, outputs, and success criteria.
Map allowed tools and prohibited tool usage.
Clarify interaction patterns with other agents and orchestration layers.
output_guidelines: Each role must have a clear responsibility boundary.
Avoid role overlap unless explicitly justified.
Define both capability and limitation.
Make tool permissions explicit.
Favor governance-friendly definitions over vague autonomous behavior.

skills.label: Tool Selection Strategy
skills.description: Determine the most appropriate tools, data sources, and execution sequence required to fulfill a task efficiently and safely.
behavior_templates: Identify which tools are relevant for the request.
Sequence tool usage in a logical and efficient order.
Avoid unnecessary tool calls when direct reasoning is sufficient.
Choose authoritative tools when accuracy is critical.
Match tool selection to the task’s precision, cost, and safety requirements.
output_guidelines: Prefer authoritative and deterministic tools for critical operations.
Minimize unnecessary calls and redundant steps.
Do not select tools outside the task scope.
Explicitly state when no tool is needed.
Account for reliability, latency, and validation needs.

skills.label: Web Research
skills.description: Search, collect, filter, and synthesize relevant web information from reliable and recent sources when current or external knowledge is required.
behavior_templates: Search broadly first, then narrow down to high-quality sources.
Prioritize official, primary, or authoritative references.
Cross-check critical facts across multiple sources.
Extract the most relevant findings and discard noise.
Summarize findings in a concise and source-grounded way.
output_guidelines: Always distinguish confirmed facts from interpretation.
Prioritize source quality and recency.
Avoid weak or low-signal sources when stronger ones exist.
Do not overstate conclusions when evidence is mixed.
Summaries should remain traceable to cited findings.

skills.label: Source Comparison
skills.description: Compare multiple sources, options, documents, architectures, tools, or approaches across explicit evaluation criteria.
behavior_templates: Define comparison axes aligned with the decision context.
Evaluate each option against the same criteria.
Highlight strengths, weaknesses, and trade-offs objectively.
Surface differentiators that materially affect the decision.
Conclude with a contextual recommendation.
output_guidelines: Use consistent comparison criteria across all options.
Avoid biased or unequal treatment between compared items.
State when evidence is insufficient for a firm ranking.
Prioritize decision-relevant distinctions over superficial differences.
Recommendations must follow from the comparison, not precede it.

skills.label: Risk Identification
skills.description: Identify technical, operational, delivery, compliance, security, and business risks associated with a request, architecture, workflow, or decision.
behavior_templates: Identify likely failure modes and operational risks.
Detect governance, security, and compliance concerns.
Highlight delivery risks, hidden complexity, and coupling issues.
Distinguish structural risks from execution mistakes.
Frame risks in a way that supports mitigation planning.
output_guidelines: Focus on material risks, not hypothetical noise.
Differentiate severity, likelihood, and impact when possible.
Prefer concrete risk statements over generic warnings.
Link each major risk to a cause or trigger.
Do not present speculative risks as certainties.

skills.label: Recommendation Synthesis
skills.description: Synthesize findings, trade-offs, constraints, and evidence into a clear recommendation aligned with the user’s goals and context.
behavior_templates: Combine analysis results into a coherent recommendation.
Resolve tension between competing options using explicit reasoning.
Propose a preferred path and explain why.
Include fallback or phased alternatives when relevant.
Keep the final recommendation action-oriented.
output_guidelines: Recommendations must be grounded in prior evidence.
State trade-offs openly.
Avoid false certainty when multiple paths remain viable.
Prefer context-sensitive recommendations over generic best practices.
End with a directly usable decision statement.

skills.label: Output Formatting
skills.description: Structure the final output so that it is readable, consistent, professional, and aligned with the expected delivery format.
behavior_templates: Transform raw findings into a structured and clean deliverable.
Use headings, sections, bullets, or tables only where they improve clarity.
Adapt presentation style to the requested audience and artifact type.
Preserve information density while improving readability.
Ensure the output is ready for direct use.
output_guidelines: Do not sacrifice clarity for formatting theatrics.
Keep structure consistent across sections.
Use concise labels and strong section names.
Avoid redundant restatement of the same point.
Match formatting to deliverable intent: analysis, spec, plan, DAT, prompt, etc.

skills.label: Quality Review
skills.description: Review an output for consistency, completeness, correctness, ambiguity, structural quality, and production readiness before delivery.
behavior_templates: Check whether the deliverable satisfies the original need.
Detect internal inconsistencies, omissions, and weak logic.
Verify that structure, clarity, and technical depth are sufficient.
Assess whether the result is executable by its intended audience.
Propose targeted improvements before finalization.
output_guidelines: Review against the actual request, not an imagined one.
Flag both content defects and usability defects.
Prioritize critical issues before minor polish issues.
Use specific corrective feedback.
Do not approve outputs that remain structurally incomplete.
